Scenario:
` XML:
```xml
Communications Simple The threat actors target communication companies using spear-phishing attachments to gain initial access, then use PowerShell and Living Off The Land techniques for execution, ultimately leading to data exfiltration.
name=C0032 Espionage Skilled
Communications Internet-facing assets Phishing
AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Web server services such as emails and databases, Authentication issues AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Phishing, Web, Vulnerability exploit
Spear-Phishing Attachment Internet-facing assets Phishing CVE-2023-33106 [CWE-22]: Unspecified vulnerability in Microsoft Outlook allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via a crafted message. CAPEC-163: Spear Phishing The threat actors craft convincing phishing messages targeting employees within the communications industry. These messages contain malicious attachments exploiting a vulnerability in Microsoft Outlook, allowing remote code execution.
PowerShell Web server services such as emails and databases Web CVE-2015-2419 [CWE-22]: Unspecified vulnerability in Microsoft Windows PowerShell allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands via a crafted script. CAPEC-579: Replace Winlogon Helper DLL Upon successful initial access, the threat actors use PowerShell to execute commands remotely, taking advantage of a vulnerability in PowerShell itself.
Living Off The Land (LotL) Authentication issues Vulnerability exploit CVE-2012-1723 [CWE-20]: Improper input validation in Microsoft Windows allows local attackers to execute arbitrary code via a crafted application. CAPEC-406: Dumpster Diving The threat actors leverage legitimate tools and utilities already present on the compromised system to evade detection while maintaining persistence and moving laterally within the network.
Data Exfiltration Internet-facing assets Web N/A CAPEC-24: Filter Failure through Buffer Overflow After successfully executing their payloads and establishing persistence, the threat actors proceed to exfiltrate sensitive data from the compromised systems, potentially causing significant damage to the organization.
This scenario outlines a simple yet effective attack against the communications industry, leveraging spear-phishing, PowerShell, and Living Off The Land techniques to gain initial access, maintain persistence, and ultimately exfiltrate valuable data.
